name: Flan Mexicano
instructions:
1. Place the White Sugar into a heavy saucepan over medium-high heat. Cook, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon or heat-resistant spatula, until the sugar melts completely and turns a rich golden amber color, about 10 minutes. Watch very carefully once the syrup begins to change color because it can burn quickly and become bitter.

2. Carefully pour the hot caramel syrup into a flan mold, tilting the mold to coat the bottom evenly. Set aside to cool and harden while you prepare the custard.

3. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).

4. Zest the Orange to get the zest, then cut and squeeze to extract the fresh Orange Juice. Place the Condensed Milk, Whole Milk, Eggs, Egg Yolks, Orange Juice, orange zest, Vanilla Extract, and Cornstarch into a blender. Blend on medium speed until completely smooth and well combined, about 1 minute.

5. Pour in the Heavy Cream and pulse several times just to incorporate without creating too many bubbles.

6. Pour the custard mixture through a fine mesh strainer over the cooled caramel syrup in the flan mold to remove any lumps.

7. Line a roasting pan with a damp kitchen towel to prevent the flan mold from sliding. Place the filled flan mold carefully on the towel in the roasting pan, then place the roasting pan on the middle oven rack.

8. Fill the roasting pan with enough boiling Water to reach halfway up the sides of the flan mold, creating a water bath for gentle, even cooking.

9. Bake in the preheated oven until the center of the flan is set but still has a slight jiggle when gently nudged, 45 minutes to 1 hour. Avoid overbaking or the texture will become grainy.

10. Remove from the oven and let cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight for best results.

11. To serve, run a sharp paring knife around the inside edge of the mold to loosen the flan. Place a serving plate with a rim on top of the mold, then quickly flip both over together. Gently lift the mold to release the flan and allow the caramel syrup to cascade beautifully over the custard.
simpleInstructions:
1. Melt the White Sugar into golden caramel and pour into a flan mold to cool.

2. Blend the custard ingredients until smooth and pour over the hardened caramel.

3. Bake the flan in a Water bath until set but slightly jiggly.

4. Cool completely, refrigerate, then unmold and serve with caramel on top.
